input.task:
Build a workflow with:
1. A Manual Trigger node
2. A Wait node set to 1 second
3. A Set node named "running state test"

Connect them in order: Manual Trigger → Wait (1 second) → Set node ("running state test")
input.conversationContext: User wants a simple 3-node workflow: Manual Trigger → Wait (1 second) → Set node named "running state test".

output.result: STOP. Direct builder calls require `bypassPlan: true` + an existing `workflowId` + a one-sentence `reason`. Use that combination for any edit to an existing workflow. For new workflows, multi-workflow builds, or data-table schema changes, call `plan` with a `build-workflow` task instead — the planner discovers credentials, data tables, and best practices, and schedules an orchestrator-run verification checkpoint.

input.summary: Build a minimal manual-trigger workflow with a Wait node (1 second) and a Set node named 'running state test'.
assumptions: Wait node duration is exactly 1 second
Set node has no field mappings — it is present for state observation purposes only

item.name: Running State Test
item.purpose: A simple test workflow triggered manually. It waits 1 second via a Wait node, then passes through a Set node named 'running state test'. Used to verify workflow running state behavior.

output.result: Added: Build 'Running State Test' workflow (1 item total)

item.title: Verify 'Running State Test' workflow runs successfully
item.instructions: Use verify-built-workflow with the work item ID from the build outcome. Confirm the workflow completes without errors, passes through the Wait node (1 second delay), and the Set node named 'running state test' appears in the output. Pass condition: execution completes with no errors and all three nodes show successful execution.
